title: introduce-guided-rag-atelier-quickstart-with-composable-adapters
preview: Motivation: Provide a compact, reproducible developer quickstart that helps engineers iterate on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) flows with composable tool adapters (HTTP, SQL, FS) and snapshot-driven end-to-end assertions. This quickstart targets local-first authoring (devcontainer + headless indexer) and a sm...
text:
Motivation: Provide a compact, reproducible developer quickstart that helps engineers iterate on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) flows with composable tool adapters (HTTP, SQL, FS) and snapshot-driven end-to-end assertions. This quickstart targets local-first authoring (devcontainer + headless indexer) and a small wasm-based reranker so teams can prototype without cloud dependencies.

What this PR adds: 1) examples/guided-atelier/ — a self-contained quickstart with stepwise authoring UI (lightweight React), a toy indexer (local sqlite + embedding shim), and three adapter examples (http_adapter.ts, sql_adapter.ts, fs_adapter.ts). 2) devcontainer/ and .devcontainer.json to run the quickstart with one-click in VS Code; the container spins up a headless indexer and a local wasm reranker. 3) docs/quickstarts/guided-atelier.md — a new quickstart page for the docs site with step-by-step...

title: Introduce compact RAG Playwright integration and local scenario recorder
preview: Motivation: Developers frequently ask for a small, reproducible harness that demonstrates RAG workflows end-to-end in a browser context while capturing deterministic telemetry for local debugging and CI. This PR introduces a lightweight Playwright integration plus a local scenario recorder that can capture step-by-s...
text: Motivation: Developers frequently ask for a small, reproducible harness that demonstrates RAG workflows end-to-end in a browser context while capturing deterministic telemetry for local debugging and CI. This PR introduces a lightweight Playwright integration plus a local scenario recorder that can capture step-by-step interactions, network stubs, and RAG tool calls for later replay or CI assertions. --- What this changes: added a new example app (examples/rag-playwright) that runs a tiny React UI and a Node-based recorder; a Playwright test suite that exercises common RAG scenarios (search->tool-call->compose->verify); devcontainer + npm scripts to run recorder and replay flows; documentation and a short Quickstart on how to record scenarios, attach telemetry to Redwood Console, and produce reproducible regression tests. --- Checklist: [x] Add example app and recorder runtime; [x] Ad...

title: Introduce stubbed tool-adapter playground and auth examples
preview: Motivation: teams frequently need a safe, repeatable way to prototype agents that call external tools (storage, calendars, webhooks, connectors) without setting up real credentials or risking side effects. This PR adds a small, documented playground that: 1) provides a plugin-style 'tool adapter' API for wiring conn...
text: Motivation: teams frequently need a safe, repeatable way to prototype agents that call external tools (storage, calendars, webhooks, connectors) without setting up real credentials or risking side effects. This PR adds a small, documented playground that: 1) provides a plugin-style 'tool adapter' API for wiring connectors to agents; 2) includes stubbed adapters for S3-like storage, Slack webhook, and Google Drive listing; 3) demonstrates a minimal OAuth/ephemeral-credential exchange shim for local development; 4) wires structured-output contracts and example prompt templates to make tool calls observable in traces and telemetry. What this changes (high level): - new examples/plugin-toolbinding/ directory with 3 example adapters and a lightweight dev server that runs them in-process - docs/examples/toolbinding.md with usage, security considerations, and contract schemas - examples/play...

title: Introduce interactive RAG simulation shell CLI with tool stubs and session export
preview: Motivation: teams repeatedly ask for a lightweight, replayable local playground to iterate on retrieval-augmented generation prompts, tool-calling behavior, and agent orchestration without spinning up full infra. This PR introduces `rag-sim`, an interactive CLI 'simulation shell' that lets authors run stepwise scena...
text:
Motivation: teams repeatedly ask for a lightweight, replayable local playground to iterate on retrieval-augmented generation prompts, tool-calling behavior, and agent orchestration without spinning up full infra. This PR introduces `rag-sim`, an interactive CLI 'simulation shell' that lets authors run stepwise scenarios, inject fake tool responses, snapshot sessions, and export deterministic seeds for CI/eval pipelines.

What this PR does: adds a small TypeScript CLI shim and example playbooks that emulate the Redwood runtime semantics (context window simulation, KV cache warmup stub, and streaming output). The shell supports step, run, rewind, and patch commands so developers can test partial tool failures and fallback logic quickly. Sessions can be exported as JSON or a compact replay script that our e2e harness can consume. The goal is low-friction iteration for authoring and regre...

title: introduce-rag-microservice-tool-calling-docker-quickstart
preview: Add a compact, docker-first RAG microservice quickstart that demonstrates function/tool-calling, streaming responses, and a minimal KV cache for low-latency private deployments. Motivation: customers frequently ask for a small, deployable reference that shows how to wire Redwood SDK into a tiny service that handles ...
text: Add a compact, docker-first RAG microservice quickstart that demonstrates function/tool-calling, streaming responses, and a minimal KV cache for low-latency private deployments. Motivation: customers frequently ask for a small, deployable reference that shows how to wire Redwood SDK into a tiny service that handles document retrieval, model orchestration, and calls external tools (search + knowledge connector) with clear observability hooks. This PR delivers: 1) examples/rag-microservice/ - a ready-to-run template (Dockerfile + docker-compose) that boots a single-node instance with an embedded retriever, Redwood SDK integration, and a simple tool interface for invoking a mock search API. 2) README quickstart that walks through local Docker run, env var configuration for hosted vs. private endpoints, and sample curl/SDK calls for streaming. 3) src/agent.ts - an opinionated agent orches...

title: Add compact RAG dependency-injection samples and browser dev proxy
preview: Motivation: Teams repeatedly ask for composable RAG patterns that are easy to reason about and test. This PR adds a compact dependency-injection (DI) oriented samples folder demonstrating how to compose retrievers, indexers, and tool-calling agents in a small, testable way. It also includes a tiny browser dev-proxy ...
text:
Motivation: Teams repeatedly ask for composable RAG patterns that are easy to reason about and test. This PR adds a compact dependency-injection (DI) oriented samples folder demonstrating how to compose retrievers, indexers, and tool-calling agents in a small, testable way. It also includes a tiny browser dev-proxy to simplify local authoring (pre-signed snippet proxy + telemetry stubs).

What this PR contains: a) an authoring README with usage patterns and mental model, b) three TypeScript sample modules that show layered DI: core client wiring, test doubles for retrievers, and a toy tool-caller adapter; c) a minimal browser proxy (node/express) that proxies indexed snippet requests and fakes auth for local dev; d) e2e smoke scripts and a lightweight smoke CI step.

Design notes: the DI layer is intentionally minimal — it uses factory functions and explicit interfaces rather than a h...

title: Introduce RAG micro-bundle and local plugin-runner for rapid prototyping
preview: Motivation: enable engineers to prototype retrieval-augmented agents and tool-calling flows locally without pulling the entire examples monorepo. The micro-bundle packages minimal adapters (HTTP tool, file-tool, open-search stub), a CLI to run a dev-mode agent, and a tiny WASM worker for sandboxed plugin logic. This...
text:
Motivation: enable engineers to prototype retrieval-augmented agents and tool-calling flows locally without pulling the entire examples monorepo. The micro-bundle packages minimal adapters (HTTP tool, file-tool, open-search stub), a CLI to run a dev-mode agent, and a tiny WASM worker for sandboxed plugin logic. This targets developer experience and workshop use-cases: lower friction to teach RAG patterns and iterate on tool integrations.

Summary: single compact example directory (examples/rag-microbundle) that can be run with `yarn dev` or the included Go CLI. The bundle includes: a) README with step-by-step quickstart, b) example prompts and prompt templates, c) local tool runner (Node + WASM shim) that demonstrates function/tool calling with an LLM stub, and d) a telemetry hint file showing recommended tracing hooks for quick troubleshooting.

Checklist: - [x] Minimal CLI scaffold ...

title: Introduce RAG plugin SDK + extensibility quickstart
preview: Context: Customers often need to extend Redwood RAG flows with custom third-party adapters (custom vector connectors, proprietary tool wrappers, or edge-specialized preprocessors). The goal of this PR is to ship a minimal, well-documented RAG Plugin SDK and a hands-on quickstart that demonstrates how to author, test...
text:
Context: Customers often need to extend Redwood RAG flows with custom third-party adapters (custom vector connectors, proprietary tool wrappers, or edge-specialized preprocessors). The goal of this PR is to ship a minimal, well-documented RAG Plugin SDK and a hands-on quickstart that demonstrates how to author, test, and publish a local plugin that integrates with Redwood tool-calling and retrieval pipeline.

Motivation: Existing quickstarts show how to wire RAG flows end-to-end but don't provide a structured pattern for third-party plugin authors (type-safe lifecycle, capability discovery, and test harness). This makes it hard for partner teams and community contributors to build resilient adapters that behave correctly under streaming and fallback conditions.

What I changed: Added a new quickstart directory (examples/rag-plugin-sdk-quickstart) containing:
- A compact RAG Plugin SDK...

title: Introduce RAG Seedkit: guided-playground CLI, flow templates, and tool-adapter spec
preview: Motivation: onboarding teams repeatedly ask for a compact, opinionated starter that demonstrates: 1) a small guided CLI that walks users through building a RAG flow, 2) authorable flow templates (decision points + tool bindings), and 3) an explicit minimal tool-adapter spec (Go + TS examples) that shows how to regis...
text:
Motivation: onboarding teams repeatedly ask for a compact, opinionated starter that demonstrates: 1) a small guided CLI that walks users through building a RAG flow, 2) authorable flow templates (decision points + tool bindings), and 3) an explicit minimal tool-adapter spec (Go + TS examples) that shows how to register tools with the agent runtime. This PR introduces the 'seedkit' quickstart under examples/seedkit and follows the same testable patterns we use across other quickstarts. Checklist:
- [x] scaffolding for guided-playground CLI (TS) with step assertions
- [x] three flow templates (basic-qna, multi-tool, sessioned-chat)
- [x] minimal tool-adapter spec and Go shim example
- [x] structured-output validators and example evals
- [x] CI smoke tests and simple unit tests for the CLI runner
Description of changes (single-string summary): Adds a new examples/seedkit package with a s...

title: Introduce async tool conductor playground with cooperative tool-runner and stepwise traces
preview: Motivation: developers need a small, focused example showing how to orchestrate multiple asynchronous tool calls from a RAG-style agent while preserving stepwise debug traces and streaming partial tool outputs back to the client. This playground demonstrates a cooperative "tool conductor" pattern: tools register as ...
text:
Motivation: developers need a small, focused example showing how to orchestrate multiple asynchronous tool calls from a RAG-style agent while preserving stepwise debug traces and streaming partial tool outputs back to the client. This playground demonstrates a cooperative "tool conductor" pattern: tools register as long-running, cancellable actors that emit intermediate events (progress, partial results, diagnostics). The conductor coordinates these actors, merges partial outputs into the agent response stream, and records compact stepwise traces for postmortem/eval.

What this PR adds: a new lightweight example under examples/async-conductor that contains: a TypeScript playground server that simulates async tools (index.ts), a small agent shim that shows how to do incremental streaming and tool fallback, README usage and tutorial, telemetry probe presets for the Console, and e2e smok...

title: Introduce RAG Playbook Simulator and Compact Authoring Kit
preview: Motivation: onboarding teams struggle to iterate on RAG playbooks without a lightweight local runner that mirrors Redwood's execution semantics. This PR introduces a compact Playbook Simulator (playbook-sim) and a small authoring kit to accelerate iterative authoring and smoke e2e testing. What it contains: 1) playb...
text: Motivation: onboarding teams struggle to iterate on RAG playbooks without a lightweight local runner that mirrors Redwood's execution semantics. This PR introduces a compact Playbook Simulator (playbook-sim) and a small authoring kit to accelerate iterative authoring and smoke e2e testing. What it contains: 1) playbook-sim/runner: a node-based simulator that interprets playbook step graphs, executes stubbed tool calls, and supports snapshot/replay for deterministic e2e checks; 2) authoring/compact-ui: a minimal web UI (React) showing step sequencing, scenario presets, and telemetry probe toggles for quick validation; 3) docs/quickstarts/playbook-sim-quickstart.md: single-page quickstart integrated into the docs site with copy-paste examples and troubleshooting notes; 4) telemetry/probes: small SDK hooks for emitting compact probes used by the Console smoke harness; 5) tests/smoke: two...

title: Introduce external-connector tool-calling samples + docs (retry/backoff & idempotency)
preview: Summary: Adds a small, focused example set and docs demonstrating best practices for tool-calling external connectors (HTTP APIs, webhooks, and database adaptors) from RAG agents and tool-calling flows. The goal is to give engineers a reference for safe external I/O: retries with jittered backoff, idempotency tokens...
text:
Summary: Adds a small, focused example set and docs demonstrating best practices for tool-calling external connectors (HTTP APIs, webhooks, and database adaptors) from RAG agents and tool-calling flows. The goal is to give engineers a reference for safe external I/O: retries with jittered backoff, idempotency tokens, short-circuiting on known transient errors, authentication patterns (token rotation + KMS/HSM hints), and a lightweight local emulator for offline testing.

Motivation and context: We've seen multiple customer support threads and internal runbooks where integrations with downstream APIs cause silent duplicates, unbounded retries, or leakage of credentials during local development (see ENG-2345). This PR collects a minimal, copy-pasteable sample set plus docs to reduce integration time and avoid common pitfalls. It also feeds a quickstart entry on the docs site so users ca...

title: Add minimal chat app template to accelerate Hosted API activation
preview: This PR adds a small, copy-pasteable "minimal chat app" example intended to reduce time-to-first-token for new Hosted API users. The goal is to provide a single folder that can be run in <5 minutes with an API key, supports streaming by default, and matches the onboarding email + console nudge CTAs ("Make your first...
text:
This PR adds a small, copy-pasteable "minimal chat app" example intended to reduce time-to-first-token for new Hosted API users. The goal is to provide a single folder that can be run in <5 minutes with an API key, supports streaming by default, and matches the onboarding email + console nudge CTAs ("Make your first request" / "Enable streaming"). It also includes lightweight telemetry guidance so the Growth funnel can attribute activation accelerators without collecting sensitive payloads.

What’s included: (1) New example at examples/minimal-chat-app/ implemented as a Node + Express server with a tiny static UI. Server proxies to Redwood Hosted API using the Redwood TS SDK, streams tokens to the browser via SSE, and shows basic error states (missing/invalid API key, rate limited). (2) README updates: top-level README now highlights the minimal chat app as the recommended first proje...

title: Add production-ready streaming chat example (retries/backoff, structured logs, health check)
preview:
Context: We’ve seen repeated onboarding + early-prod issues around streaming disconnects and “we hit 429 immediately” surprises (SUP-18488 / SUP-18510). As part of the onboarding checklist work, we want a reference example that is explicitly production-oriented (not just a minimal demo).

This PR adds a Node.js + Ty...
text:
Context: We’ve seen repeated onboarding + early-prod issues around streaming disconnects and “we hit 429 immediately” surprises (SUP-18488 / SUP-18510). As part of the onboarding checklist work, we want a reference example that is explicitly production-oriented (not just a minimal demo).

This PR adds a Node.js + TypeScript streaming chat example that shows: (1) server-side proxying of streaming responses (so API keys are not shipped to the browser), (2) safe retry/backoff patterns for rate limits and transient gateway errors, (3) structured JSON logging that is usable in production, and (4) a simple health check endpoint for container orchestration.

What’s included (high-level): - New example: examples/streaming-chat-prod-ready - Express server with endpoints: GET /healthz (liveness/readiness), POST /v1/chat/stream (SSE streaming proxy) - Redwood API call uses streaming mode and for...

title: Route experiments sample app: sticky key support + variant header debugging
preview:
Summary: Adds an updated route experiments sample app demonstrating (1) passing a stable stickiness key to ensure deterministic variant assignment and (2) reading Redwood experiment/variant response headers to debug routing in the field.

Context: With route-level experiments rolling into Console Rollouts, multiple ...
text:
Summary: Adds an updated route experiments sample app demonstrating (1) passing a stable stickiness key to ensure deterministic variant assignment and (2) reading Redwood experiment/variant response headers to debug routing in the field.

Context: With route-level experiments rolling into Console Rollouts, multiple beta customers asked for a concrete end-to-end example showing what to send from clients and what to log when assignment looks skewed. Support also reported confusion about which headers to inspect during triage (see SUP-24831).

What’s included (high level):
- Adds an explicit `stickyKey` input to the sample UI and server routes.
- Sends stickiness key using the request header `x-redwood-sticky-key` (preferred) with a safe fallback to a stable application-level identifier when available.
- Logs and displays the experiment assignment response headers:
 - `x-redwood-experime...

title: Add agent tooling example with safe defaults (guardrails + structured logs)
preview: Context: As part of the RAG + agents quickstart workstream, we want examples that demonstrate “production-minded by default” patterns (safe defaults, guardrails, and operational hooks) without forcing users into a full template scaffold. This PR adds a new agent tooling example that shows (1) a bounded agent loop (m...
text: Context: As part of the RAG + agents quickstart workstream, we want examples that demonstrate “production-minded by default” patterns (safe defaults, guardrails, and operational hooks) without forcing users into a full template scaffold. This PR adds a new agent tooling example that shows (1) a bounded agent loop (max iterations + max wall time), (2) strict tool schema + tool output size limits, (3) resilient request settings (timeouts + retry/backoff for non-streaming calls; streaming is out-of-scope here), and (4) structured logs with correlation IDs and redaction. What’s included: - New example at examples/agents/tooling-safe-defaults/ with a small HTTP server exposing POST /agent/run and a couple of demo tools (search_docs + get_account_status). - Agent loop with guardrails: max_iterations (default 6), max_tool_calls (default 10), max_tool_output_bytes (default 32KB), per-request ...
